ddls:
CREATE TABLE "indicators" (
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each indicator */,
  "indicator_name" TEXT /* Name of the indicator */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the indicator */,
  "indicator_type" TEXT /* Type of indicator (e.g., health, education, socioeconomic) */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the indicator */,
  "description_long" TEXT /* Longer description of the indicator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "continent_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the continent where the country is located */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the country is located */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code for the country */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_countries_continent_id FOREIGN KEY ("continent_id") REFERENCES continents ("continent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "continents" (
  "continent_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each continent */,
  "continent_name" TEXT /* Name of the continent */,
  "continent_code" TEXT /* Code for the continent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("continent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "continent_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the continent where the region is located */,
  "region_code" TEXT /* Code for the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_regions_continent_id FOREIGN KEY ("continent_id") REFERENCES continents ("continent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "years" (
  "year_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each year */,
  "year_name" TEXT /* Name of the year */,
  "year_description" TEXT /* Description of the year */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_values" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data value */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the indicator for which the data is collected */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country for which the data is collected */,
  "year_id" INTEGER /* ID of the year for which the data is collected */,
  "value" REAL /* Actual value of the indicator */,
  "trend" REAL /* Trend of the indicator value over time */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data */,
  "confidence_interval" TEXT /* Confidence interval for the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_values_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES indicators ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_values_year_id FOREIGN KEY ("year_id") REFERENCES years ("year_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_values_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ranks" (
  "rank_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rank */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the indicator for which the rank is calculated */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country for which the rank is calculated */,
  "year_id" INTEGER /* ID of the year for which the rank is calculated */,
  "rank" INTEGER /* Rank of the country based on the indicator value */,
  "rank_type" TEXT /* Type of rank (e.g., highest, lowest, average) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rank_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ranks_year_id FOREIGN KEY ("year_id") REFERENCES years ("year_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ranks_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ranks_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES indicators ("indicator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "lmic_deciles" (
  "lmic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each LMIC decile */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country for which the LMIC decile is calculated */,
  "decile" INTEGER /* LMIC decile of the country */,
  "lmic_decile_type" TEXT /* Type of LMIC decile (e.g., income, education) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("lmic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_lmic_deciles_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, researcher, analyst) */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization affiliated with the user */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the indicator being accessed */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country being accessed */,
  "year_id" INTEGER /* ID of the year being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download, edit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES indicators ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_year_id FOREIGN KEY ("year_id") REFERENCES years ("year_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data request */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the request */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the indicator being requested */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country being requested */,
  "year_id" INTEGER /* ID of the year being requested */,
  "request_date" TEXT /* Date when the request was made */,
  "request_status" TEXT /* Status of the request (e.g., pending, approved, rejected) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_requests_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_requests_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_requests_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES indicators ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_requests_year_id FOREIGN KEY ("year_id") REFERENCES years ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_downloads" (
  "download_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data download */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user downloading the data */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the indicator being downloaded */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country being downloaded */,
  "year_id" INTEGER /* ID of the year being downloaded */,
  "download_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was downloaded */,
  "download_format" TEXT /* Format of the downloaded data (e.g., CSV, Excel, PDF) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("download_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_downloads_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_downloads_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_downloads_year_id FOREIGN KEY ("year_id") REFERENCES years ("year_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_downloads_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES indicators ("indicator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the indicator related to the notification */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country related to the notification */,
  "year_id" INTEGER /* ID of the year related to the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., data update, new indicator) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES indicators ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_year_id FOREIGN KEY ("year_id") REFERENCES years ("year_id")
)
